text.title: API Gateway Patterns Procedure
text.summary: 5-step procedure to apply API Gateway Patterns end-to-end. Each step has named input, action, and output. Decision gates reference rules from `01-core-rules.xml`.

step.name: pick-gateway-and-mode
step.input: Traffic shape, region count, dynamic-config requirement
step.action: Apply r6 + r7 to choose Kong (DB-less for static, DB for dynamic), AWS API Gateway (multi-region/serverless), Envoy (mesh), or nginx (expert ops). Record the choice as an ADR.

step.name: design-stateless-gateway
step.action: Configure the gateway as stateless (r1): JWT in tokens, rate-limit state in Redis, no business logic in plugins. Route business aggregation to a separate BFF service.

step.name: wire-cross-cutting-concerns
step.input: Auth provider, X-Forwarded-For policy, request-id, timeouts
step.action: Configure per-consumer rate limiting (r2), X-Request-ID propagation (r3), gateway-shorter-than-client timeouts (r4), and per-service circuit breakers (r5).

step.name: add-smoke-tests
step.action: Pair every route change with a smoke test (curl script or Pact contract) that proves route, auth, rate-limit behave; commit smoke tests beside the gateway YAML.

step.name: validate-and-deploy
step.action: Run `scripts/validate-api-gateway-patterns.py --file gw-config.json --self-test`; deploy via GitOps; verify smoke tests green.

section.title: Decision gates
section.p: Between steps, consult `content/06-decision-tree.xml` if the observed signal does not match expected output. The decision tree always terminates at a rule id from `01-core-rules.xml` or at `skip-this-methodology`.
